summary.body: Computer Engineering undergraduate at UFS with experience in full-stack development, test automation, CI/CD, and applied research. I build software with focus on maintainability, delivery quality, and measurable impact, combining product validation with engineering practices.

bullet_points: Built a React Native (Expo) and Spring Boot mobile MVP for pilot launch, implementing five core flows: authentication, campaign creation, donations, donation history, and voucher creation/recovery.

Reduced repetitive manual validation effort, measured by automated coverage of 63 end-to-end scenarios across core product flows, by implementing E2E tests with Maestro and WireMock and incorporating new flows into the regression suite.
Reduced operational risk in the payment flow, measured by eliminating dependency on a single provider, by integrating multiple payment providers into the system.

Reduced release and deployment effort, measured by cutting the process from about 1 hour of manual work to a 15-minute automated pipeline, by setting up CI/CD with GitHub Actions and Terraform-managed AWS infrastructure.

Built a plagiarism-detection evaluation dataset, measured by 500 original source codes, 250 in C and 250 in Python, plus obfuscated versions, by modeling the dataset and generating transformed samples with open-source obfuscation tools.

Reduced manual structural analysis effort, measured by automated generation of reversible syntax-tree JSON representations, by implementing a Python-based Tree-sitter converter for code comparison workflows.

Improved same-language plagiarism detection performance, measured by an 86.36% F1-score for Python and a 68.63% F1-score for C, by applying comparison algorithms to the generated JSON representations.
Served as a senior-member coach during training, helping the team qualify for the 2025 first phase and place 280th out of 984 teams.

Built an LLM routing module for multiple AI APIs using LangChain and PostgreSQL as a vector database, reducing inference cost by routing prompts to the smallest adequate model instead of a fixed default; won 2nd place at the ChipLabs Hackathon.
